currentUse: Affordable ownership housing — the former Cambridge Lithuanian church and rectory at 424-430/432 Windsor Street were converted by Just A Start Corporation into the 14-unit Windsor Street Condominiums (completed Fall 2012, all units sold and occupied), with the Lithuanian-inscribed Virgin Mary relief preserved on the facade of the city-landmarked building.
summary: No Lithuanian parish named St. Peter (Šv. Petro) appears to have existed in Cambridge, MA: the litua.com directory of Lithuanian churches in the USA lists Šv. Petro only in South Boston (75 Flaherty Way, still active), and the St. Peter Parish that does exist in Cambridge (100 Concord Ave) is a territorial parish founded in 1848 with no Lithuanian heritage, still open today. The Lithuanian parish of Cambridge — and the one whose fate matches this archive record's notes exactly — was Immaculate Conception Lithuanian Church at 430-432 Windsor Street, built circa 1910-1913 in Mission Style with traditional Lithuanian architectural elements. That parish closed in 2004 during the Archdiocese of Boston's mass reconfiguration; the archdiocese sold the property in 2007 to the nonprofit Just A Start Corporation. The church and rectory were designated a Cambridge protected landmark (they appear on the city's official Designated Cambridge Landmarks list), and the adaptive reuse into 14 affordable ownership condominiums was completed in Fall 2012 with historic details retained; all units were sold to income-eligible first-time homebuyers and are occupied. The principal surviving Lithuanian element is the Virgin Mary relief over the entrance bearing the pre-modern Lithuanian inscription "Lietuviu Rymo Kataliku Bažnyčia Nekalto Panos Marijos prasidejimo," and the 1972 rectory by Lithuanian architect Eugenijus Manomaitis also incorporates Lithuanian design motifs. No recent press, preservation campaigns, or Lithuanian community reunions tied to the Cambridge building were found.

developments.headline: Cambridge Lithuanian parish (Immaculate Conception) closed
developments.detail: The Immaculate Conception Lithuanian church at 432 Windsor Street, built as the Lithuanian parish of Cambridge, was closed in 2004 — the year of the Archdiocese of Boston's sweeping parish reconfiguration under Archbishop Sean O'Malley.

sources.title: Boston, Massachusetts: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage

developments.headline: Archdiocese of Boston sold the church property to Just A Start Corporation
developments.detail: The archdiocese sold the church and rectory at 424-430 Windsor Street in 2007 to the Cambridge affordable-housing nonprofit Just A Start Corporation. No sale price was published in the sources reviewed.

sources.title: Windsor Street Condominiums (424-430 Windsor Street)
sources.publisher: City of Cambridge Community Development Department

sources.title: Boston, Massachusetts: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage

developments.headline: Church and rectory designated a protected Cambridge landmark
developments.detail: The Immaculate Conception Church and Rectory, 430-432 Windsor Street, was designated a historic landmark by the City of Cambridge and appears on the Cambridge Historical Commission's official list of Designated Cambridge Landmarks. The designation protected the building's character, including its Lithuanian facade elements, through the housing conversion.

sources.title: Windsor Street Condominiums (424-430 Windsor Street)
sources.publisher: City of Cambridge Community Development Department

developments.headline: Conversion to 14 affordable ownership condominiums completed
developments.detail: Just A Start completed the adaptive reuse of the former church and rectory into the Windsor Street Condominiums: 14 ownership units (1 one-bedroom, 6 two-bedroom, 6 three-bedroom, 1 four-bedroom), all sold to first-time homebuyers earning up to 100% of area median income, with occupancy in Fall 2012 and 'many historic details' maintained. Funders included Cambridge Savings Bank, the Cambridge Affordable Housing Trust, City of Cambridge HOME funds, and the Harvard 20/20/2000 Loan Fund.

sources.title: Windsor Street Condominiums (424-430 Windsor Street)
sources.publisher: City of Cambridge Community Development Department

developments.headline: Lithuanian facade inscription survives on the converted building
developments.detail: The Virgin Mary relief over the entrance still displays the pre-modern Lithuanian inscription 'Lietuviu Rymo Kataliku Bažnyčia Nekalto Panos Marijos prasidejimo,' and the cornerstone records the first Lithuanian pastor Krasnickas; the adjacent 1972 rectory by architect Eugenijus Manomaitis carries Lithuanian roof-design elements.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: MAJOR NAME CONFLICT: the archive record identifies the Cambridge, MA Lithuanian parish as 'Šv. Petro,' but no St. Peter Lithuanian parish in Cambridge could be found in any source. The litua.com directory of Lithuanian churches in the USA lists Šv. Petro only in South Boston (75 Flaherty Way — a Lithuanian parish that is still OPEN, contradicting 'closed' if that parish were meant). The St. Peter Parish that exists in Cambridge (100 Concord Ave) was founded in 1848, is territorial/non-Lithuanian, and is still active — also contradicting the record. The archive record's descriptive notes ('converted to apartments with Lithuanian facade inscriptions preserved as a landmark requirement') match, in every particular, the Immaculate Conception (Nekalto Prasidėjimo) Lithuanian Church at 430-432 Windsor Street, Cambridge: closed 2004, sold by the archdiocese 2007, city-landmarked, converted to 14 condominiums in 2012 with the Lithuanian facade relief preserved. The record's ownership (diocese_rc), status (closed), and ending (diocese_closed) are all consistent with Immaculate Conception Cambridge. Recommend correcting the parish name in the canonical record to Nekalto Prasidėjimo (Immaculate Conception), Cambridge, MA, or verifying against the original Draugas citations. Minor refinement: the conversion is ownership condominiums (affordable homeownership), not rental apartments.
gaps: Identity: could not locate any source attesting a 'Šv. Petro' Lithuanian parish in Cambridge (searched English and Lithuanian forms, litua.com directory, Notre Dame parish histories, Archdiocese of Boston church lists) — this record therefore documents the building the archive notes describe (Immaculate Conception Lithuanian, 432 Windsor St), flagged as a probable misnaming rather than a guess silently adopted. Exact parish closure date within 2004 and the final-Mass details were not found in an opened source (2004 comes from Global True Lithuania). Sale price of the 2007 archdiocese-to-Just A Start transfer was not published in any source reviewed. The precise landmark designation date (reported as April 2, 2009 in search snippets of a Cambridge City Clerk order that could not be opened — server refused connection) is given only as 'circa 2009'; the landmark status itself is verified via the official CHC landmarks list. Construction date conflict between opened sources: Global True Lithuania says built 1913, the Cambridge Historical Commission memo says built 1910. Parish founding date not established. No recent (2020s) press, diocesan announcements, preservation campaigns, or Lithuanian community reunions relating to the Cambridge building were found; Draugas searches surfaced no Cambridge-specific coverage.
